title: prangos heyniae
intro: Prangos heyniae is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant, native to the mountainous regions of Turkey, Iran, and Iraq. It is a member of the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is also known as Turkish angelica due to its medicinal properties. Prangos heyniae has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
